meta.title: Solar Panel Cleaning Robot Row-Transfer Docking Station, Taypro CRADYL
meta.description: Taypro CRADYL is a battery-powered movable docking station for utility-scale solar plants in India. Autonomous row-to-row transfer on end-mounted rail tracks lets one cleaning robot service multiple rows on scattered plants. IP65, solar-charged, cloud-connected.
meta.openGraphTitle: CRADYL Row-Transfer Docking for Solar Cleaning Robots | Taypro India
meta.openGraphDescription: Launched movable docking station: autonomous robot transfer between rows, rail-based travel, solar-charged lithium battery, LTE/Wi-Fi fleet integration with NECTYR.
meta.openGraphImageAlt: Taypro CRADYL row-transfer docking station on rail tracks at a utility-scale solar plant in India
meta.twitterTitle: CRADYL Solar Robot Docking Station | Taypro
meta.twitterDescription: Movable docking station for autonomous row-to-row solar cleaning robot transfer. Built for scattered utility plants in India.

schema.productName: CRADYL, Autonomous Row-Transfer Docking Station for Solar Cleaning Robots
schema.productDescription: Taypro CRADYL is a battery-powered movable docking station that autonomously transfers solar panel cleaning robots between rows on end-mounted rail tracks, enabling multi-row coverage with a single robot on scattered Indian utility-scale plants.
schema.howToName: How to transfer a solar cleaning robot between rows with Taypro CRADYL
schema.howToDescription: Step-by-step autonomous row transfer using the Taypro CRADYL movable docking station on end-row railing tracks, from robot return and docking through rail navigation, alignment, and redeployment on the next row.

howItWorks.title: How does CRADYL transfer a cleaning robot between rows?
howItWorks.subtitle: Once railing is installed and aligned at row ends, CRADYL automates the full pick-up, transit, alignment, and redeployment cycle with no panel modifications required.

0.name: Robot completes cleaning and returns to CRADYL
0.text: When a solar row cleaning cycle finishes, the Taypro cleaning robot returns to the CRADYL docking station at the end of the row and drives into the cradle for secure locking before transit.

1.name: Docking and locking for transit
1.text: CRADYL secures the robot in the docking cradle with sensor-verified locking, protecting the unit during rail travel and in high-wind conditions when stationary.

2.name: Rail navigation to the next row
2.text: The self-propelled platform travels along end-row railing tracks at up to 5 metres per minute to the next designated row, with edge-detection sensors preventing derailment.

3.name: Sensor-guided alignment with the target row
3.text: Integrated sensors stop CRADYL precisely aligned with the new row endpoints so the cleaning robot can deploy onto the correct module line without operator adjustment.

4.name: Undocking and redeployment on the new row
4.text: The robot is released from the cradle to continue its waterless cleaning cycle on the new row while CRADYL waits for the next transfer or returns per your fleet schedule.

5.name: Cloud scheduling and fleet visibility
5.text: Movement, position, charge level, and fault diagnostics are scheduled and monitored through the remote portal with LTE and Wi-Fi connectivity, repeating per your plant cleaning programme.

cycle.0: When a row is cleaned, the robot returns to CRADYL and locks into the cradle for transit.
cycle.1: CRADYL travels along the end-row railing to the next designated row.
cycle.2: Sensor-guided positioning stops precisely aligned with the target row.
cycle.3: The robot is released to continue cleaning on the new row.
cycle.4: Movement and schedules repeat per your cleaning cycles via the cloud portal.

0.title: Autonomous Row-to-Row Transfer
0.description: Eliminates manual lift-and-shift between arrays, cutting labour hours and transfer downtime on multi-block Indian utility plants.

1.title: Single Robot, Multiple Rows
1.description: Stretch one cleaning robot across more rows per shift where capex for a robot per contiguous block is not justified.

2.title: End-Row Rail Travel
2.description: Precision-aligned railing at row ends, no modifications to solar panels, modular installation on RCC or ballast foundations.

3.title: Solar-Charged Battery Power
3.description: Dedicated onboard solar panel charges the lithium-ion pack for self-sustaining daily operations under standard daylight.

4.title: Sensor-Guided Docking
4.description: Integrated sensors align pick-up and drop-off precisely with each row for secure robot transfer and redeployment.

5.title: High Wind Resistance
5.description: Designed to hold and operate robots when docked in wind zones up to 180 km/h, and travel safely up to 40 km/h in motion.

6.title: Cloud-Connected Fleet Ops
6.description: LTE and Wi-Fi integration with NECTYR for movement scheduling, position tracking, charge visibility, and maintenance alerts.

7.title: IP65 Weather Protection
7.description: Critical systems enclosed for dust, moisture, and harsh field conditions on Indian utility-scale sites up to 90°C operating temperature.

featuresSection.title: Inside the CRADYL movable docking station
featuresSection.subtitle: Every subsystem, from rail drive to docking cradle to cloud telemetry, is built for reliable autonomous transfers on real-world scattered solar portfolios.

0.title: Rail-Based Linear Mobility
0.body: CRADYL travels on dedicated end-row railing tracks mounted on rigid ground columns, ensuring smooth, level transit between rows without crossing roads, drains, or buffer land that block continuous robot paths.

1.title: Secure Robot Pick-Up and Drop-Off
1.body: Automated docking and undocking with secure locking holds Taypro cleaning robots during transfer, reducing fall risk and protecting modules and equipment compared with ad-hoc manual moves.

2.title: Remote Monitoring and Control
2.body: Fully integrated with Taypro's remote monitoring platform: schedule movements, track position, review sensor data and battery charge in the cloud portal alongside existing robot cleaning cycles.

3.title: Modular Site Installation
3.body: No changes to solar panels themselves. After columns and railing are installed and aligned, the CRADYL unit is placed on the track and connected to the robot control system for rapid deployment.

4.title: Anti-Derailment Safety
4.body: Edge-detection sensors prevent derailment during rail travel. Enclosed drive and control systems protect against dust ingress and environmental exposure in desert and coastal Indian conditions.

5.title: Scheduled Preventive Maintenance
5.body: Maintenance activities are performed on a scheduled basis to eliminate breakdowns and sustain operational life, with fault diagnostics and alerts routed through the cloud system.

6.title: Broad Robot Compatibility
6.body: Docking compatibility with Taypro Model-A and other Taypro cleaning robots in the field, including natural pairing with HELYX pick-and-place fleets and GLYDE autonomous rows.

7.title: Slope and Temperature Tolerance
7.body: Operates on sites with up to 18° slope and maximum operating temperatures up to 90°C, matching the demanding envelope of utility-scale solar plants across India.

installation.title: How CRADYL is installed on your plant
installation.subtitle: Typical deployment follows Taypro's movable docking station installation methodology used on live Indian utility sites.
installation.item0: End-row railing tracks are mounted on columns fixed to the ground with RCC foundations or ballast, depending on geotechnical conditions.
installation.item1: Column height and alignment are managed so rail travel remains smooth and level across the transfer path.
installation.item2: The CRADYL unit is placed on the aligned track and connected to the robot control and cloud monitoring system.
installation.item3: No modifications are required on solar modules, enabling quick, modular setup once civil and rail work is complete.

useCasesSection.title: Where CRADYL fits best on Indian solar plants

0.title: Scattered utility blocks
0.body: Multi-block plants where rows are separated by roads, drainage, or buffer land and one robot cannot traverse continuously.

1.title: Semi-automatic robot fleets
1.body: Sites running HELYX or similar pick-and-place robots that need faster inter-row movement without adding headcount.

2.title: CAPEX optimisation programmes
2.body: Owners who want autonomous cleaning coverage but need to defer buying additional robots until transfer automation proves ROI.

3.title: Utility-scale retrofit programmes
3.body: Existing Taypro robot deployments where end-row rail and CRADYL extend reach without replacing the cleaning robot fleet.

indianConditions.title: Built for Indian utility-scale scattered plants

faqSection.title: Frequently asked questions
faqSection.subtitle: Common questions about Taypro CRADYL row-transfer docking for solar cleaning robots in India.

0.question: What is Taypro CRADYL?
0.answer: CRADYL is Taypro's autonomous row-transfer movable docking station — a battery-powered platform on end-row rails that moves a solar cleaning robot between rows. It is the fleet-integrated platform name used on taypro.in today.

1.question: How does a solar panel cleaning robot row-transfer docking station work?
1.answer: After a row is cleaned, the robot returns to CRADYL and locks into the cradle. CRADYL travels along end-row railing to the next row, aligns using integrated sensors, releases the robot to clean the new row, and repeats on schedule. Movement and status are visible in the cloud monitoring portal with LTE or Wi-Fi connectivity.

2.question: Which Taypro cleaning robots are compatible with CRADYL?
2.answer: CRADYL is designed for Taypro Model-A and compatible Taypro cleaning robots, including fleets already using HELYX semi-automatic pick-and-place robots and GLYDE automatic robots on scattered or multi-block layouts. Confirm rail layout, row spacing, and robot model with Taypro engineering before procurement.

3.question: Does CRADYL require modifications to solar panels?
3.answer: No modifications to solar modules are required. Installation uses dedicated end-row railing tracks on columns fixed with RCC foundations or ballast. Once rails are aligned, the CRADYL unit is placed on the track and connected to robot control and cloud systems.

4.question: Is CRADYL available in India now, or is it coming soon?
4.answer: CRADYL is a launched Taypro product shipping for Indian utility-scale plants. It is not a roadmap or pilot-only offering. Contact Taypro with your plant layout for integration planning, compatibility confirmation, and commercial quotation.

5.question: What are the key specifications of the CRADYL docking station?
5.answer: Representative specifications include a 1000 mm × 4000 mm footprint, 220 kg weight, rail-based travel up to 5 m/min, solar-charged lithium-ion battery, IP65 rating, LTE/Wi-Fi cloud connectivity, up to 180 km/h wind resistance when docked, 40 km/h in motion, 18° max slope, and 90°C max operating temperature. Site-specific rail design may adjust details.

6.question: When should I choose CRADYL instead of buying another cleaning robot?
6.answer: CRADYL is strongest on scattered plants where one robot could clean more rows if transfers were automated, but buying a second robot for each non-contiguous block is uneconomical. If you have large contiguous arrays needing daily unattended cleaning, a fully automatic robot such as GLYDE may be the primary answer; CRADYL complements scattered layouts.

7.question: How is CRADYL monitored and scheduled?
7.answer: CRADYL integrates with Taypro's remote monitoring platform. Operators can schedule movement, track position, and review sensor data and battery charge levels in the cloud portal alongside existing robot cleaning schedules on NECTYR.

8.question: What maintenance and support does Taypro provide for CRADYL?
8.answer: Taypro performs scheduled preventive maintenance on docking stations and rails, provides remote diagnostics through the cloud system, and targets same-day on-site intervention across India when physical service is required, consistent with Taypro's robot fleet AMC and support model.

9.question: How do I get pricing for CRADYL at my solar plant?
9.answer: Pricing depends on number of transfer paths, rail civil scope, robot models in use, and AMC coverage. Share MW capacity, row spacing, block layout, and current robot fleet with Taypro for a site-specific quote and deployment plan.
